title: Safety Net or Growth?
scenario: Your emergency fund sits at about ₹40,000, roughly one and a half months of expenses. You have ₹10,000 spare this month. You are torn between finishing the emergency fund first or jumping straight into a market-linked investment for higher returns.

choices.label: Top up the emergency fund toward three to six months of expenses first
choices.lesson: An emergency fund is the foundation. Investing before it is built can force you to sell at a loss when a sudden expense hits.

choices.label: Put the ₹10,000 straight into a volatile investment for higher returns
choices.lesson: Higher returns mean higher swings. Without a cash buffer, a market dip plus an emergency can corner you into bad timing.
